items.title: CMS warns states: federal Medicaid/CHIP matching limits for certain noncitizens start Oct. 1, 2026
items.summary: CMS issued guidance preparing states for a statutory change that limits when states can claim federal matching funds for Medicaid and CHIP for people who are not U.S. citizens/nationals or who fall into specific noncitizen categories. The change begins October 1, 2026, with exceptions including continued federal matching for emergency medical conditions and for states’ option to cover lawfully residing children and pregnant people in Medicaid/CHIP.

items.title: HUD reverses EHV/Stability Voucher flexibilities on SSN and immigration-document verification
items.summary: HUD Notice PIH 2026-05 revises Emergency Housing Voucher (EHV) and Stability Voucher (SV) requirements by removing a waiver that allowed PHAs to admit families without Social Security number or eligible immigration-status documentation at intake (with up to 180 days to provide it). PHAs must follow the standard verification rules in 24 CFR 5.508 (citizenship/immigration status) and 24 CFR 5.210–5.218 (SSNs), with a 60-day compliance timeframe described in the notice.

items.title: HUD rescinds expedited homelessness-admission verification waivers for HCV, PBV, and public housing
items.summary: The National Low Income Housing Coalition reports HUD rescinded PIH 2024-17 (via PIH 2026-04), which had created a streamlined process for certain regulatory waivers to expedite admissions for people experiencing homelessness. NLIHC also notes PIH 2026-05 removed EHV/SV flexibilities that let PHAs delay collecting SSN and citizenship documentation for up to 180 days after admission.

items.title: USDA posts expanding list of SNAP waivers restricting purchases (e.g., soda/candy) with 2026 rollout dates
items.summary: USDA’s SNAP ‘Food Restriction Waivers’ page lists approved state waivers that restrict purchases of certain items such as soda, candy, energy drinks, and desserts. Examples include Florida (target implementation 04/20/26) restricting soda/energy drinks/candy/prepared desserts and Arkansas (target implementation 07/01/26) restricting soda and certain sweetened drinks and candy.

items.title: USDA publishes SNAP FY 2026 cost-of-living adjustment (COLA) information
items.summary: USDA’s Food and Nutrition Service published a SNAP FY 2026 COLA page that states benefit standards for federal fiscal year 2026. These annual updates affect SNAP maximum allotments, deductions, and net-income limits that states use to calculate monthly benefits.

items.title: IRS reminder: EITC income cap for 2025 tax year is $68,675
items.summary: An IRS Tax Tip for the 2026 filing season notes the Earned Income Tax Credit helps low- to moderate-income workers and families and highlights an income limit for eligibility. The IRS says the maximum income amount for claiming the EITC for tax year 2025 is $68,675, with credit amounts varying by income, family size, and filing status.

items.title: IRS: 2026 filing season opened Jan. 26; many EITC/ACTC refunds expected by March 2
items.summary: The IRS opened the 2026 tax filing season on Jan. 26, 2026 and began accepting tax-year 2025 individual returns. The IRS also said most Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) and Additional Child Tax Credit (ACTC) refunds should be available by March 2, 2026 for people using direct deposit (with ‘Where’s My Refund?’ expected to show projected deposit dates for most early EITC/ACTC filers by Feb. 21, 2026).

items.title: USDA WIC memo sets FY 2026 cash-value fruit/vegetable benefit levels
items.summary: USDA’s WIC Policy Memorandum #2026-2 sets monthly cash-value voucher/benefit amounts for fruits and vegetables for FY 2026: $26 for children, $48 for pregnant and postpartum participants, and $52 for fully/mostly breastfeeding participants. The memo also notes state agencies must authorize at least one additional form of fruits and vegetables (frozen, canned, and/or dried) in addition to fresh by April 2026.

items.title: SSA: Social Security and SSI benefits rise 2.8% in 2026 (plus updated SSI federal payment standard and SGA limits)
items.summary: SSA’s 2026 COLA fact sheet reports a 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment for 2026 and provides updated program parameters, including an SSI federal payment standard of $994/month for an individual and $1,491/month for a couple. The fact sheet also lists 2026 disability-related thresholds such as Substantial Gainful Activity of $1,690/month for non-blind individuals ($2,830/month for blind individuals).
